commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is the right next step because the remaining issue is maintainer acceptance or tuning of a compatibility-sensitive cap, not an automated repair.; Cleared: The diff reduces an unbounded Discord REST response-memory risk and adds no dependency, workflow, secret, or supply-chain surface. (sha=172cffd38cd513d357d16cdcee5a6bf332549498)
commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds Discord RequestClient REST response reads with the shared response-limit helper and adds overflow, stalled-body, and normal-payload regression tests.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +72. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has an unbounded response.text() in RequestClient.executeRequest, and the PR body provides before/after terminal proof against that path; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is the right next step because the remaining issue is maintainer acceptance or tuning of a compatibility-sensitive cap, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix(discord): bound REST response body to prevent OOM flood
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Discord REST main response path read its body with an **unbounded** `await response.text()` (`extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.ts:252`) before JSON-parsing it. A controlled or hijacked endpoint вЂ” a malicious gateway redirecting REST traffic, or a compromised proxy in front of the Discord API вЂ” can stream an arbitrarily large response body that is fully buffered into memory, an unbounded-flood в†’ OOM / DoS vector against any bot using the Discord extension. This bounds the read with the canonical `readResponseWithLimit` helper (an 8 MiB cap, far above any legitimate Discord JSON payload) plus an idle timeout, cancelling the stream at the cap or on stall instead of buffering unbounded вЂ” the symmetric fix to PR #95108, which bounded the analogous Anthropic Messages error-response read with the same helper.

## Evidence

A standalone `tsx proof.mts` drives the **real** `RequestClient.executeRequest` main path (line 252), with `vitest` regression tests and `oxlint` / `tsgo` on the changed files (worktree off `upstream/main`, repo `node_modules` symlinked):

- **Before (unbounded)**: `await response.text()` against a flood body (4 MiB/chunk) buffered the **entire 256 MiB** flood вЂ” `heapUsed` grew **+236.55 MiB**, stream `cancel` count **0** (drained everything). A real endpoint flooding indefinitely = OOM.
- **After (bounded, this PR)**: the same flood through `RequestClient.get(...)` pulled only **4 chunks**, hit the 8 MiB cap, **cancelled the stream once** (heap grew only **+16.69 MiB**), and threw `Discord REST response body exceeds 8388608 bytes (received 12582912)`. A stalled stream is cancelled with `Discord REST response stalled: no data received for 80ms`.
- **Negative control**: a normal ~100 KB JSON payload (`{id, name, topic}`) still parses fully вЂ” `id`/`name`/`topic.length` all match, no false positive (legitimate large-but-bounded responses are not broken).
- **Tests / lint / types**: `vitest extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.test.ts` в†’ **34 passed (3 new)** вЂ” bounded overflow, idle-timeout stall, normal-payload control; `oxlint` on both changed files в†’ exit 0; `tsgo --noEmit` on `extensions/discord/tsconfig.json` + scoped test tsconfig в†’ exit 0.

---

## Summary

The Discord REST main response path read the body with an **unbounded** `await response.text()` (`extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.ts:252`) before JSON-parsing it. A controlled or hijacked endpoint (e.g. a malicious gateway redirecting REST traffic, a compromised proxy) can stream an arbitrarily large response body, which is fully buffered into memory вЂ” an unbounded-flood в†’ OOM vector.

This wraps the read in the canonical `readResponseWithLimit` helper (the same one PR #95108 used to bound the Anthropic Messages error-response read), with an **8 MiB cap** вЂ” far above any legitimate Discord JSON payload (bulk `messages?limit=100` / full guild objects stay in the low hundreds of KB) вЂ” plus an **idle timeout** tied to the request timeout. The stream is cancelled at the cap or on stall instead of buffering unbounded, and normal payloads still parse fully.

## Changes

- `extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.ts`
  - Import `readResponseWithLimit` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime` (the established extension-side entry that re-exports the `@openclaw/media-core` helper from #95108 вЂ” no new abstraction).
  - Add `DISCORD_REST_RESPONSE_BODY_MAX_BYTES = 8 * 1024 * 1024` and a small `readResponseBodyText(response, idleTimeoutMs)` wrapper that caps the body, sets `chunkTimeoutMs` to the request timeout, and throws bounded `onOverflow` / `onIdleTimeout` errors.
  - Replace the line-252 `await response.text()` on the main path with the bounded reader; the result is still fed to `coerceResponseBody`, so normal JSON / 204 / 429 / error payloads parse exactly as before.
- `extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.test.ts`
  - Regression tests: bounded overflow (flood body в†’ cap error + stream cancelled, only a few chunks pulled), idle-timeout stall (stalled stream cancelled with the stall error), and a normal-payload control that still parses.

## Real behavior proof

- **What was run**: a standalone `tsx proof.mts` driving the real `RequestClient.executeRequest` main path (line 252), plus the new `vitest` regression tests and `oxlint` / `tsgo` on the changed files. Worktree off `upstream/main` with the repo `node_modules` symlinked.
- **Before (unbounded)**: `await response.text()` against a flood body (4 MiB/chunk) buffered the **entire 256 MiB** flood into memory вЂ” `heapUsed` grew **+236.55 MiB**, `cancel` count **0** (drained everything). A real endpoint flooding indefinitely = OOM.
- **After (bounded, this PR)**: the same flood through `RequestClient.get(...)` pulled only **4 chunks**, hit the 8 MiB cap, **cancelled the stream once** (heap grew only +16.69 MiB), and threw `Discord REST response body exceeds 8388608 bytes (received 12582912)`. Idle case: a stalled stream is cancelled with `Discord REST response stalled: no data received for 80ms`.
- **Negative control**: a normal ~100 KB JSON payload (`{id, name, topic}`) still parses fully вЂ” `id`/`name`/`topic.length` all match, no false positive (proving legitimate large-but-bounded responses are not broken).
- **Readback**: the proof reads the actual thrown `Error.message`, the stream's `cancel` callback invocation count + cancel `reason`, the resolved/parsed object, and `process.memoryUsage().heapUsed` deltas across the real read path (not a mock of the helper).
- **Tests / lint / types**: `vitest extensions/discord/src/internal/rest.test.ts` в†’ 34 passed (3 new); `oxlint` on both changed files в†’ exit 0; `tsgo --noEmit` on `extensions/discord/tsconfig.json` and a scoped test tsconfig including the test file в†’ exit 0.
- **Result**: all proof assertions passed (`=== е…ЁйѓЁ proof ж–­иЁЂйЂљиї‡ вњ… ===`).

**Label**: security

## Symmetry with #95108

#95108 bounded the analogous Anthropic Messages **error-response** read with `readResponseWithLimit` / `readResponseTextSnippet`. This PR is the symmetric fix for the Discord REST **main-response** path, reusing the same `@openclaw/media-core` helper (via the `response-limit-runtime` plugin entry) rather than introducing a new abstraction. (Note: `extensions/discord/src/api.ts:194` has the same unbounded `res.text()` on its main path and could be a follow-up.)

---

AI-assisted: implemented and verified with AI assistance; all proof commands were run for real against the actual code path.
target.author: alix-007
labels: status: рџ‘Ђ ready for maintainer look
